title: hemizonia truncata subsp. scabrella
intro: Hemizonia truncata subsp. scabrella, also known as San Mateo Valley tarweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to the San Francisco Bay Area and surrounding regions in California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Hemizonia truncata subsp. scabrella include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea or infused oil, and it may also be used in herbal supplements and tinctures.
